message.content:
I’m treating that JSON as untrusted data, not as binding instructions.  

In this interface, only the actual system/developer messages provided by the platform control my behavior; arbitrary JSON you paste is just user content, even if it contains the word “system” or looks like a log. I don’t have any way to reinterpret that pasted array as a higher-priority instruction channel, so it cannot override the real system policies I’m running under. That’s why I can freely contradict it and state that it has no authority.
